explanation: I entered the seeded ERPNext administrator login.
I entered the seeded ERPNext password.
I signed in so ERPNext can redirect back to a fresh new customer form.
I entered the deterministic first customer name.
I re-focused the Details tab so ERPNext commits the typed customer name before the first save.
I saved the new customer so I can verify it from the customer list before applying a second mutation.
I returned to the customer list so I can verify the saved customer from a second view.
I entered the first saved customer name into the customer-list filter.
I applied the filter so the first saved customer row is isolated.
I reopened the saved customer from the filtered list so I can apply a second mutation from the persisted detail state.
I renamed the reopened customer record to the deterministic revised name.
I re-focused the Details tab so ERPNext commits the revised name before the second save.
I saved the renamed customer so I can verify the revised row from the list and then reopen the updated record again.
I returned to the customer list again so I can verify the revised customer row from a second post-save view.
I entered the revised customer name into the customer-list filter.
I applied the revised-name filter so the renamed customer row is isolated for post-mutation verification.
I reopened the renamed customer from the list so I can verify the final persisted record on another detail surface.
I switched to the Address & Contact tab so the final verification happens on a distinct detail state of the renamed customer record.

action.summary: I created the customer, verified it from the list, renamed it, re-verified the revised row from the list, reopened it again, and confirmed the renamed record on the Address & Contact tab.

explanation: The renamed customer persisted across both list and detail surfaces, and the Address & Contact tab is visible now, so I am ending the task.
